title: The Standing Crop Memory

features.name: unharvested rows
features.desc-on-touch: You stand in grain that dreams of never being cut. Each stalk remembers the season it was planted as the season it will always be planted.

features.name: harvest-date stone
features.desc-on-touch: A boundary marker carved with dates that change each time you turn away. The year written here is never the year you remember reading.

features.name: root-memory chamber
features.desc-on-touch: Beneath the soil, a cross-section reveals roots that write in the language of seasons: spiral rings encoding harvests that never happened.

features.name: fence-line division
features.desc-on-touch: The fence marks where memory stops being individual and becomes the field's. On either side, the same grain grows different histories.

features.name: seed-paper archive
features.desc-on-touch: Pressed between paper, seeds that remember being held before they were scattered. The archive that grows itself, one grain at a time.

inscription: standing grain remembers being seed; seed remembers being field; the crop dreams backward to before harvest learned to name itself

file.name: Standing_Grain_Phenology_1967_2024.txt
file.note: Harvest dates recorded annually, but each entry is dated from a different year—the document reads backward and forward simultaneously, a calendar that forgot its own passage.

exits.label: back through the kitchen door toward spice-scent and yeast-memory
deeper into grain that has begun to remember being bread
exits.to: NEW: the mill where grain dreams of becoming flour

exits.label: toward the seed-stocks where nothing has been planted yet
down to the soil-layer where roots teach each other to read
